title: SessionMeta
description:
Descriptor of one session (the append-only event log it names is stored
separately by a `SessionStore`).

agent_id.description:
Which agent implementation owns this session
(`"native"`, `"claude-code"`, ...).

base_cwd.description:
The original project directory before isolation redirected [`Self::cwd`]
to a workspace root. Lets resume fall back if the workspace is gone and
tells integration where to merge back. `None` when not isolated.

cwd.description:
Where this session's tools operate. When isolation is active this is
the workspace (worktree) root, not the original project directory —
see [`Self::base_cwd`].

depth.description:
Spawn-tree depth of this session: 0 for a root/main session, else the
parent session's depth + 1. Used to enforce a role's `max_depth` so
subagents cannot spawn indefinitely deep trees.

isolation.description:
Isolation posture this session was created with. `None` = legacy /
never isolated.

parent_id.description: Set for subagent sessions; links the session tree.

provider_session_id.description: The backing agent's own session id, for native resume.

role.description: The role this session serves (e.g. `searcher`); `None` = main.

workspace_id.description:
Identifier of the provisioned workspace when isolation is active;
`None` when the session runs directly in [`Self::cwd`].

IsolationPolicy.description:
Whether a root session runs inside an isolated working copy, and how
strictly that is required.

oneOf.description: Never isolate; the session works directly in the base directory.

Isolate when possible; fall back to the base directory (with a warning)
if a workspace can't be provisioned.

Isolation is mandatory; session creation fails if a workspace can't be
provisioned.

ModelRef.description:
Reference to a model, optionally provider-qualified:
`"anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5"` or a bare `"claude-sonnet-4-5"`.

SessionId.description: Identifies one session: an append-only event log owned by one agent.
